title: helianthemum myrtifolium subsp. origanifolium
intro: Helianthemum myrtifolium subsp. origanifolium, also known as Rockrose or Labrador Tea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in herbal preparations, due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include relief from coughs, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health. Herbal preparations of Helianthemum myrtifolium subsp. origanifolium include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
